reviews.review_msg: I failed and I'm going to retake the course, take my advice: (1) start the homework super early. It's easy marks and you will need them. (2) Same with the quizzes (3) flashcards, study daily (4) the second you don't understand something, look it up. Notation is hard at first.

Andrei is a decent prof for 210. His lectures are detailed and provide examples and extra practice from textbooks. His assignments are good in-terms of understanding the concepts and for exam practice. His exams are hard but with the assignment questions and lecture slides, you can easily pass. The TA's were fair for marking but had their moments.

Andrei was a tough grader for his midterms, but he definitely made up for it with his grading of the final. Part of the midterm difficulty came from the lack of time given, which is why the final was more fair. You definitely need to study a lot for this class, but if you do, it will pay off.

Do the homework and you can pass this class easily. Do attend lectures, he can explain concepts well. The homework is a bit challenging, but with some practice they are intuitive. Some textbook questions were in his midterms. Overall I would take 210 again with Andrei. Midterms were roughly doable, except our second midterm due to time crunch.
The final average was around 40%, so yeah… it was pretty brutal. Way harder than the midterm, not even comparable. Definitely be ready for a tough final.
